pattern: Reduce to the jump-game interval-cover problem: for each position record the farthest right it can reach, then greedily extend coverage using the tap that reaches farthest from the current window.
hints: Each tap at position i with range r covers [i-r, i+r]; translate this to: if you can reach position max(0, i-r) you can jump to at least i+r.
Build an array reach[] where reach[j] = max right endpoint reachable from position j, then apply the classic minimum-jumps greedy.
Scan left to right: maintain the current covered boundary and the farthest reachable from within it; each time you must advance beyond covered, open one tap (or return -1 if farthest hasn't grown).

edge_inputs.description: single tap with range covering the full garden
no tap has any range — garden cannot be watered
every tap covers only itself, all adjacent — exactly n taps needed
gap in the middle that no tap can cover
overlapping ranges but only one is needed

stress.description: random garden of size up to 500 with random tap ranges
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    ranges = [rng.randint(0, min(100, n)) for _ in range(n + 1)]
    return [n, ranges]
